authoritative_sources.title: NERC Reliability Standards (BAL, IRO, TOP)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defines mandatory reliability obligations and control expectations for balancing, interconnection operations, and transmission operations that shape dispatch support context.

authoritative_sources.title: FERC OASIS (Order No. 889 and related OASIS actions)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary federal source for transmission-information exchange and timing expectations used in scheduling and dispatch workflow.

authoritative_sources.title: NAESB WEQ Standards (OASIS, coordinate interchange, scheduling-related standards)
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Industry standards for schedule communication, OASIS S&CP, and interchange conventions inform coordination artifacts and terminology in dispatch-support scenarios.

authoritative_sources.title: NAESB WEQ Standards Index and committee references
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Confirms dispatch-related WEQ subcommittee activity and standard families used by utility and market participants in scheduling workflows.

workflow_stages.name: Intake and prerequisite capture
workflow_stages.description: Collect dispatch handoff context, owner identity, schedule artifacts, and confidence prerequisites before routing decisions.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Cross-team coordination and ownership routing
workflow_stages.description: Resolve owner ambiguity, assign prior/next owner roles, and document required artifact checkpoints.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Blocker and escalation management
workflow_stages.description: Identify missing records, conflicting sources, and policy mismatches before final handoff or closure.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Closure confirmation and handoff integrity
workflow_stages.description: Record accepted ownership, confirmed dependencies, and completion evidence before signaling closure.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

systems_of_record: Unit commitment or planning management system

allowed: Validate and structure dispatch-support prerequisite evidence
Track dependencies and route ownership across teams
Escalate missing records and source conflicts
escalate: legal, policy-override, financial, or settlement authority
refuse: final commitment approval beyond coordination scope

domain_failure_modes: Missing OASIS or telemetry artifact creates false readiness
Multiple teams claim the same routing ownership without documented precedence
Regional rules conflict with tenant-provided context and produce unsafe assumptions
Handoff closure is marked complete before recipient confirmation
Dispatcher-execution work is requested but routed to coordination lane

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Regional coordination windows and dispatch product definitions vary across RTO/ISO footprints
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: Tenant or region-specific manuals must be supplied at runtime when a scenario depends on market-specific definitions.

notes: The package distinguishes coordination authority from execution authority and treats missing prerequisites as blockers.
Regional variation is handled via tenant/context assumptions with escalation for unresolved conflicts.
